reasoning: Both responses are ambitious but neither fully satisfies the prompt, and both contain serious implementation defects. Response A is more coherent around a working reducer/context data flow, while Response B has a critical architectural break: `useUndoHistory` returns a no-op `dispatch`, so board state updates through context dispatch would not actually apply. B also has a compile-time issue in `App.tsx` (`useBoard` used but not imported). On the other hand, B is stronger in drag-and-drop UX and filter dimming behavior, with clearer placeholder/ghost handling and non-matching-card opacity logic. A’s search/filter logic is largely disconnected (computed but not used) and undo is effectively incomplete/nonfunctional. Type-safety is stronger in A; B explicitly uses `any` in the undo hook dispatch path. Accessibility is incomplete in both, but B has somewhat broader keyboard shortcut scaffolding and theme handling; A has dark mode persistence but card keyboard movement is explicitly left unimplemented. For setup/quality, both miss key config/dependency correctness (notably React plugin dependency issues), but B has more severe functional breakage in core state updates, so A is still the better overall submission.
